blocks.content: Davenport came back downstairs at one-fifteen. He had his coat on.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Alright. Here's where we are. We're treating this as an unexplained death pending examination by the medical examiner. That means I'd like all of you to remain available — either here or somewhere local — for the next forty-eight hours. If any of you had plans to leave tomorrow, I need you to hold off on that.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: We don't have a determination yet. That takes time. For now, we treat the situation with appropriate care and thoroughness.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: It means we don't jump to conclusions. It means we do this right.

blocks.speaker: Sophie
blocks.content: Could it have been his heart? His blood pressure—

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: That's one possibility being considered, yes. The medical examiner will be thorough.
He looked at each of them. Long enough to be certain, not long enough to be searching.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Is there anything else any of you want to tell me tonight? Anything you remembered after we spoke, or anything you didn't think to mention?
Silence. Five people with everything and nothing to say.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Alright. Deputy Briggs will be outside tonight. Try to get some rest.
He headed for the front door. Put his hat on. Paused with his hand on the frame.
He meant it. He always meant it. It never got easier to mean it and that was probably a good sign.
